checks.name: covers_feature
checks.evidence: AC1 asserts state.metamask.lastVisitedPerpsRoute is populated with a /perps path and a fresh timestamp after navigating to the Perps tab. AC2 asserts the background setLastVisitedPerpsRoute(null) action clears the entry. AC3 asserts that on home remount the persisted entry is consumed (cleared) — proving the wiring that consumes the saved path on reopen.

checks.name: strict_assertions
checks.evidence: All eval_sync nodes use operator eq with explicit values; wait_for uses operator truthy on boolean conditions. No contains.

checks.name: uses_existing_flows
checks.evidence: setup-unlock calls extension-core/unlock-wallet; ac1-navigate-perps calls perps/navigate-perps-tab. No hand-rolled steps for covered ops.

checks.name: visual_evidence
checks.evidence: evidence-persisted-after-visit.png captures the Perps tab after state is persisted; evidence-home-consumed-entry.png captures the Home surface after persisted entry is consumed. Proof is primarily state-based (stateHooks assertions); screenshots corroborate the user-visible surfaces.

checks.name: teardown_safe
checks.evidence: teardown-clear resets lastVisitedPerpsRoute to null so later recipes start from a clean baseline.

notes: Live validation: 13/13 nodes pass on CDP 6661 against rebuilt dev bundle on feat/tat-2691-persist-perp-reopen-state. Screenshots captured under artifacts/screenshots/.
Auto issue review status: review (1 non-gating React unmount warning from PerpsView/PerpsViewStreamBoundary — pre-existing, unrelated to this change).
AC1 navigates to /perps/market/BTC via the shared perps/navigate-to-market-detail flow (not the Home perps tab, which lives at '/' with a ?tab=perps query and does not mount PerpsLayout).
